message:
chore(asupersync): re-baseline tokio guard ledger after facade migration

Re-baseline all invariant max_counts in the tokio exception ledger to
reflect actual remaining usage after the fcp-async-core facade migration:

- tokio_refs_total: 159 -> 102
- tokio_test_attributes: 109 -> 0 (all migrated)
- tokio_sync_channels: 4 -> 35 (now counted as facade channel usage)

Ratchet schedule phases updated with actual from/to counts and notes
explaining remaining references are in fcp-async-core facade or
differential_regression tests.

Also includes beads issue metadata updates for tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -503,16 +503,16 @@
       "id": "tokio_refs_total",
       "pattern": "tokio::",
       "scope": ["crates", "connectors", "tests", "fuzz"],
-      "max_count": 159,
+      "max_count": 102,
       "owner_bead": "flywheel_connectors-1ud0u.4",
       "expires_on": "2026-03-21T00:00:00Z",
-      "reason": "Block any net-new Tokio references while migration removes existing surface"
+      "reason": "56 in fcp-async-core + 2 doc comments + 44 in differential_regression.rs (intentional raw tokio comparison tests)"

       "id": "tokio_test_attributes",

       "reason": "Block new Tokio async tests until shared ASUPERSYNC test harness lands"

       "expires_on": "2026-03-21T00:00:00Z",
       "reason": "Prevent growth of local runtime bootstrap points"

     },
